subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 11
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering geography, nature, science, health, and technology for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: Which of these animals can live on both land and in water?
questions.explanation: Frogs are amphibians, which means they spend part of their life in water and part on land.
questions.hint: They start as tadpoles in water.

questions.q: What is the largest country in South America?
questions.explanation: Brazil is the largest country in South America by both land area and population.
questions.hint: It is famous for the Amazon Rainforest.

questions.q: Which part of the plant grows underground and holds it in place?
questions.explanation: Roots anchor the plant in the soil and absorb water and nutrients.
questions.hint: They are usually hidden under the dirt.

questions.q: What is the main source of vitamin D that we get from being outside?
questions.explanation: Our bodies produce vitamin D when our skin is exposed to sunlight.
questions.hint: It comes from the bright star in the sky.

questions.q: Which of these is a liquid state of matter?
questions.explanation: Water is a liquid at room temperature, while ice is a solid and steam is a gas.
questions.hint: You drink it when you are thirsty.

questions.q: Who is responsible for teaching students in a classroom?
questions.explanation: A teacher helps students learn new subjects and skills.

questions.q: Which planet is famous for its beautiful rings?
questions.explanation: Saturn is the sixth planet from the Sun and is most famous for its spectacular ring system.
questions.objective: Understand basic facts about the solar system.

questions.q: Which of these is a healthy snack choice?
questions.explanation: Carrot sticks are a vegetable and contain vitamins that are good for your eyes and body.

questions.q: What instrument is used to measure temperature?
questions.explanation: A thermometer is used to measure how hot or cold something is.

questions.q: Which gas do plants release during the day?
questions.explanation: During photosynthesis, plants release oxygen into the air, which humans and animals need to breathe.
questions.hint: It is the gas we breathe in to live.
